title: Listening - Part 4: Joining a Local Choir
description: You are going to read a magazine article about an intellectual process known as critical thinking. For questions 31 – 36, choose the answer (A, B, C or D) which you think fits best according to the text. Mark your answers on the separate answer sheet.
topic: five people talking about their reasons for joining a community choir and the impact it had

content.audio_script: Speaker 1: I’d always loved singing in the shower, but [21]I joined the choir primarily because I was feeling quite lonely after moving to a new city where I didn't know anyone[/21]. I needed a social outlet. [26]The most unexpected outcome has been the massive improvement in my confidence, not just in singing but in speaking up during work meetings[/26]. It turns out that standing on a stage and performing for an audience makes giving a presentation to a board of directors feel easy. It’s given me a voice I didn't know I had. || Speaker 2: I’m a high school teacher, and [22]I joined the choir as a way to manage the intense stress and emotional demands of my job[/22]. I needed a place where I could just let go. Singing in a group is incredibly therapeutic. [27]What I didn't expect was how much the breathing techniques we use in choir would help me stay calm in the classroom[/27]. When a student is being difficult, I now use the same deep, controlled breathing to keep my composure. It’s been a physical and emotional lifesaver that has made me a much more patient educator. || Speaker 3: I’ve always been a fan of classical music, so [23]I joined a choir because I wanted to understand the technical side of how complex harmonies are built[/23]. I wanted an intellectual challenge. [28]The biggest hurdle for me has been the frustration of learning to read sheet music at sixty[/28]. My brain just doesn't seem to process the dots on the page as quickly as the younger members. It’s been a very humbling experience, but the sense of achievement when I finally nail a difficult passage is incomparable. || Speaker 4: I recently retired and [24]I joined the choir because I wanted to honor the memory of my mother, who was a professional singer and always wanted me to take it up[/24]. It’s been a very emotional journey. [29]The most rewarding aspect has been the deep sense of belonging I feel when our voices all blend together[/29]. In that moment, you aren't an individual anymore; you’re part of something much larger and more beautiful. It’s given me a sense of spiritual connection that I haven't found anywhere else, and it’s helped me process my grief in a very positive way. || Speaker 5: I’m a firm believer in the power of local communities, so [25]I joined the choir because I wanted to help raise money for the town’s heritage fund through our performances[/25]. I wanted to be useful. [30]The most significant result for me has been the discovery of a diverse group of people I never would have met otherwise[/30]. We have members from eighteen to eighty, from all walks of life. It’s broadened my horizons and challenged my assumptions about my neighbors. It’s made me feel much more integrated into the life of the town, and I’ve made some truly wonderful friends in the process.

task1.title: Task One
task1.instruction: For questions 21–25, choose from the list (A–H) what primarily motivated each speaker to join the choir.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 joined because she was lonely after moving to a new city and needed a social outlet.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 joined to manage the intense stress and emotional demands of his job.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 wanted to understand the technical side of complex harmonies and have an intellectual challenge.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 joined to honor her mother's memory and fulfill her wish.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 joined to help raise money for the town's heritage fund through performances.

options.A: to improve their physical lung capacity
options.C: to support a local charitable cause
options.D: to prepare for a professional audition
options.G: to gain a better grasp of musical theory
options.H: to expand their social circle in a new area

task2.title: Task Two
task2.instruction: For questions 26–30, choose from the list (A–H) what each speaker mentions as an outcome of their experience.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 mentions a massive boost in confidence that helped her at work.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 mentions how breathing techniques help him stay calm in the classroom.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 found it frustrating and challenging to learn to read sheet music.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 highlights the deep sense of belonging and spiritual connection.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 mentions meeting a diverse group of people and feeling more integrated.

options.A: a realization that they have a talent for solo work
options.B: a feeling of being part of a larger entity
options.C: the difficulty of attending regular rehearsals
options.D: an increase in their self-assurance at work
options.E: the pleasure of performing in large venues
options.F: the transfer of new skills to their professional life
options.G: the struggle to master a new technical skill
options.H: an appreciation for the diversity of their community
